description: Phase 1: Add model/difficulty/escalationNote data layer + pure model resolution logic. Enables PRD-level default model, per-task overrides, difficulty-based escalation, synergy cluster analysis, and crash-recovery model escalation — all as pure testable functions and round-trip-safe data fields.

criteria: Rust: No warnings in `cargo check` output
Rust: No warnings in `cargo clippy` output
Rust: All tests pass with `cargo test`
No breaking changes to existing APIs unless explicitly required
globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks

mergeStrategy: Merge to main after MILESTONE-FINAL passes. Squash commits optional.

priorityPhilosophy.description: The hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN - Anticipate edge cases. Tests verify boundaries work correctly
2. FUNCTIONING CODE - Pragmatic, reliable code that works according to the plan
3. CORRECTNESS - Code compiles, type-checks, passes all tests deterministically
4. CODE QUALITY - Clean code, good patterns, no warnings
principles: Ship solid working code first, then use tests to improve it
Test the boundaries and exceptions - edge cases are where bugs hide
Handle Option/Result explicitly; avoid unwrap() in production code

priorityGuidelines.medium: 21-50: Fix in current phase if time permits

acceptanceCriteria: Test model_tier: opus/sonnet/haiku substrings map to correct tiers, None and unknown strings return Default
Test resolve_task_model: task.model wins over difficulty, difficulty 'high' forces opus when no task model, prd_default used as fallback, all-None returns None
Known-bad discriminator: test that difficulty 'medium' does NOT escalate to opus (rejects naive 'any difficulty = opus' impl)
Test resolve_iteration_model: highest tier wins in synergy cluster, empty touchesFiles yields single-task cluster
Test escalate_model: haiku->sonnet, sonnet->opus, opus->opus, None->None
Test file compiles and all tests pass
userStories.description: Write tests BEFORE implementing model.rs. Cover model_tier(), resolve_task_model(), resolve_iteration_model(), escalate_model() with happy path, edge cases, and known-bad discriminators.
edgeCases: model_tier with empty string '' should return Default, not panic
model_tier with 'claude-opus-4-6' vs just 'opus' - both should match Opus
resolve_task_model with task_model=Some('') (empty string) - should it be treated as None or pass through?
resolve_iteration_model with zero pending tasks - selected task alone determines model
escalate_model with unknown model string like 'gpt-4' - should return same string unchanged

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Only exact 'high' triggers escalation, not 'High' or 'HIGH'

failureModes.cause: Empty touchesFiles on both selected and candidate tasks
failureModes.expectedBehavior: No overlap found, cluster is just selected task

invariants: resolve_task_model(Some(x), _, _) == Some(x) for any non-None x (task model always wins)
escalate_model output tier >= input tier (never downgrades)
resolve_iteration_model output tier >= resolve_task_model output for selected task (cluster can only upgrade)
userStories.notes: TDD: Write these tests first in src/loop_engine/model.rs #[cfg(test)] mod tests. Tests should define expected behavior for the pure functions that will be implemented in FEAT-001.
userStories.title: Initial tests for model resolution pure logic

acceptanceCriteria: Test migration v6 up: columns added to tasks and prd_metadata tables, schema_version = 6
Test migration v6 down: schema_version reverts to 5
Test insert_task with model/difficulty/escalation_note: values stored and retrievable
Test insert_prd_metadata with default_model: value stored and retrievable
Test PrdFile/PrdUserStory parsing: JSON with model/difficulty/escalationNote fields deserializes correctly
Test backward compat: JSON without model/difficulty fields parses with None defaults
Known-bad discriminator: test that escalationNote uses camelCase in JSON (not snake_case)
userStories.description: Write tests for schema migration v6 (model/difficulty/escalation_note columns) and JSON round-trip (parse -> import -> export preserves fields).
edgeCases: Migration on empty database (fresh install, no existing tasks)
Migration on database with existing tasks (columns added with NULL default)
Export with all new fields NULL: fields should be omitted from JSON (skip_serializing_if)
Import with escalationNote but no difficulty: both stored independently

failureModes.cause: ALTER TABLE on a table with existing rows
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Existing rows get NULL for new columns, no data loss

failureModes.cause: TryFrom<Row> on pre-v6 database (columns don't exist)

invariants: Migration is idempotent - running v6 on a v6 DB is a no-op
All new columns are nullable - INSERT without them succeeds
JSON round-trip: parse(export(import(json))) == parse(json) for new fields
userStories.notes: TDD: Tests in src/db/migrations/tests.rs for migration, and integration test for round-trip. Round-trip test: create JSON string -> parse PrdFile -> insert -> export -> verify fields match.
userStories.title: Initial tests for migration v6 and data round-trip

acceptanceCriteria: ModelTier enum with Default(0), Haiku(1), Sonnet(2), Opus(3) variants deriving Ord, PartialOrd, Eq, PartialEq
Constants: OPUS_MODEL, SONNET_MODEL, HAIKU_MODEL matching claude-loop.sh values
model_tier(Option<&str>) -> ModelTier: substring match on opus/sonnet/haiku
resolve_task_model(task_model, difficulty, prd_default) -> Option<String>: precedence task > high-difficulty > prd_default > None
resolve_iteration_model(selected_id, selected_files, task_models) -> Option<String>: synergy cluster + highest tier
escalate_model(current) -> Option<String>: haiku->sonnet->opus->opus, None->None
pub mod model; added to src/loop_engine/mod.rs
All TEST-INIT-001 tests pass
userStories.description: Create src/loop_engine/model.rs with ModelTier enum, model constants, and pure functions: model_tier, resolve_task_model, resolve_iteration_model, escalate_model. Register as pub mod model in mod.rs.
userStories.notes: Pure logic only, no DB or I/O. This is the core of the feature. Follow patterns in crash.rs and stale.rs (pure state machines with no I/O).
userStories.title: Pure model resolution module (model.rs)

acceptanceCriteria: v6.rs follows v5.rs pattern: Migration struct with version, description, up_sql, down_sql
up_sql: ALTER TABLE tasks ADD COLUMN model TEXT, difficulty TEXT, escalation_note TEXT; ALTER TABLE prd_metadata ADD COLUMN default_model TEXT
down_sql: UPDATE global_state SET schema_version = 5 (columns left in place for SQLite compat)
mod.rs: CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION = 6, mod v6 added, &v6::MIGRATION in MIGRATIONS array
All TEST-INIT-002 migration tests pass
Existing v5 databases upgrade cleanly
userStories.description: Create src/db/migrations/v6.rs adding model TEXT, difficulty TEXT, escalation_note TEXT to tasks table and default_model TEXT to prd_metadata table. Bump CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION to 6.
userStories.notes: Follow exact pattern of v5.rs. All new columns must be nullable (TEXT without NOT NULL). The up_sql must include UPDATE global_state SET schema_version = 6.
userStories.title: Database migration v6: model/difficulty columns

acceptanceCriteria: PrdFile has: #[serde(default)] pub model: Option<String>
PrdUserStory has: #[serde(default)] pub model: Option<String>
PrdUserStory has: #[serde(default)] pub difficulty: Option<String>
PrdUserStory has: #[serde(default)] pub escalation_note: Option<String>
Existing PRD JSON files without these fields parse without error
New JSON with model/difficulty/escalationNote deserializes correctly
userStories.description: Add model: Option<String> to PrdFile, and model/difficulty/escalation_note to PrdUserStory in parse.rs. All fields use #[serde(default)] for backward compatibility.
userStories.notes: serde(rename_all = camelCase) is already on both structs, so escalation_note in Rust maps to escalationNote in JSON automatically. All new fields MUST use #[serde(default)] to maintain backward compat.
userStories.title: Parse structs: add model/difficulty/escalationNote fields

acceptanceCriteria: Task struct has: pub model: Option<String>, pub difficulty: Option<String>, pub escalation_note: Option<String>
All three use #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
Task::new() initializes all three as None
TryFrom<&Row> reads with .ok().flatten() pattern (handles pre-v6 DBs where columns don't exist)
Existing tests that construct Task still compile
userStories.description: Add model, difficulty, escalation_note optional fields to the Task struct and update TryFrom<&Row> to read them gracefully from the database.
userStories.notes: Follow the pattern of blocked_at_iteration/skipped_at_iteration fields which use the same .ok().flatten() approach for optional columns.
userStories.title: Task model: add model/difficulty/escalation_note fields

acceptanceCriteria: insert_task: INSERT includes model, difficulty, escalation_note columns (3 new params)
update_task: UPDATE SET includes model, difficulty, escalation_note columns (3 new params)
insert_prd_metadata: INSERT includes default_model column (1 new param from prd.model)
All params read from PrdUserStory/PrdFile struct fields
Existing tests that call these functions still pass
NULL values stored when fields are None
userStories.description: Update insert_task, update_task, and insert_prd_metadata in import.rs to persist the new fields from PrdUserStory/PrdFile to the database.
userStories.notes: Callers (in init/mod.rs) don't change — they pass PrdUserStory/PrdFile which now has the new fields. Only the SQL queries and params arrays change.
userStories.title: Import: add model/difficulty/escalation_note to INSERT/UPDATE

acceptanceCriteria: ExportedPrd has: #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")] pub model: Option<String>
ExportedUserStory has: model, difficulty, escalation_note (all Option<String>, skip_serializing_if)
PrdMetadata has: pub default_model: Option<String>
load_prd_metadata SELECT includes default_model column
load_tasks SELECT includes model, difficulty, escalation_note columns
Exported JSON omits fields when NULL, includes them when present
Full round-trip test passes: JSON -> init -> export -> fields preserved
userStories.description: Update ExportedPrd, ExportedUserStory, PrdMetadata, and the load functions in export/prd.rs to include model/difficulty/escalation_note fields.
userStories.notes: ExportedUserStory uses #[serde(rename_all = camelCase)] so escalation_note auto-maps to escalationNote. Use skip_serializing_if = Option::is_none for clean JSON output.
userStories.title: Export: add model/difficulty/escalationNote to exported JSON

acceptanceCriteria: NextTaskOutput has: model, difficulty, escalation_note (all Option<String>, skip_serializing_if)
build_task_output() populates from scored_task.task.model/.difficulty/.escalation_note
build_task_json() includes model/difficulty/escalationNote in JSON when present
task-mgr next JSON output shows new fields when present
Existing tests that construct NextTaskOutput updated to include new fields
userStories.description: Add model, difficulty, escalation_note to NextTaskOutput and populate them in build_task_output(). Update build_task_json() in prompt.rs to include these in the task JSON block.
userStories.notes: build_task_json is in prompt.rs. It conditionally adds optional fields like description/notes. Follow the same pattern for model/difficulty/escalation_note.
userStories.title: NextTaskOutput: add model/difficulty/escalation_note fields

acceptanceCriteria: No unwrap() in production code paths
All SQL queries match column count with params count
All serde annotations correct (camelCase, skip_serializing_if, default)
TryFrom<Row> handles missing columns gracefully (no panic on pre-v6 DB)
All new pub functions/structs are reachable from production code paths
No unused code warnings for new fields/functions
Any issues found have corresponding CODE-FIX-xxx tasks added to JSON
userStories.description: Review all implementation tasks for code quality, error handling, correct SQL queries, serde annotations, and verify all new code is wired in.
userStories.notes: Review complete. No issues found - all 7 acceptance criteria pass. No CODE-FIX tasks needed. Verified: no unwrap() in prod, SQL column/param counts match, serde annotations correct, TryFrom graceful, all pub items reachable, no unused warnings.
userStories.title: Review Phase 1 implementation for quality and integration

acceptanceCriteria: No code duplication (DRY principle)
Functions under 30 lines (flag complex ones)
No tight coupling between modules
Code is easy to change and extend
Any issues found have corresponding REFACTOR-1-xxx tasks added to JSON
userStories.description: Analyze implementation code for DRY violations, complexity, coupling, and maintainability issues before testing phase.
userStories.notes: Review complete. No refactoring needed. All Phase 1 functions are under 30 lines, no DRY violations introduced, zero coupling in model.rs (pure functions), code follows established patterns and is easy to extend. Pre-existing long functions in import.rs/export/prd.rs are long due to SQL column count, not complexity — outside Phase 1 scope.
userStories.title: Review implementation for refactoring opportunities
